preamble: Affrication. The palatal nasal [ɲ] develops into a voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ]. This shift is often found in word-initial or emphatic positions where a nasal consonant gains a stop-like onset and subsequent fricative release.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
